data.title: Oracle's unit wins $3.31B defense software contract with an upside option
data.description:
• Oracle Corporation (ORCL) announced on Thursday that its Oracle America unit won a $3.31B U.S. Department of War Enterprise Software Initiative contract.
  • The agreement has a five-year ordering period and supports software purchases across the Department of War, the intelligence community, and the U.S. Coast Guard.
  • The contract covers Oracle software licenses, cloud services, support, consulting, and related IT services.
  • The award includes an unexercised option, which, if exercised, could increase the total contract value to $6.99B.
  • No funds are obligated at award, with funding to be provided through individual task orders as requirements arise.
  • The Naval Information Warfare Center Pacific is the contracting activity.

data.title: Stewart anticipates full-year revenue up 20% and earnings up 30% while existing home sales growth tops around 2%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stewart Information Services Corporation (STC) Q2 2026

Management View

  • "I am very pleased with the second quarter results. We sustained our growth momentum in each of our business lines and strengthen our future earnings outlook by significantly investing in some additional business opportunities." (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)

  • "Year-to-date, we have grown revenues by 26% and grew adjusted pretax income by 45%, all while the housing market remains at multi-decade lows." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "Earnings growth for the quarter was 13% with slower growth driven by a decision to make some significant additional investments in individuals and teams to boost our organic growth initiatives in 3 of our title businesses." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "In the quarter, we made additional investments in individuals and teams of around $8 million to capture these targeted business opportunities." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "However, we are currently working on transactions that we anticipate will close in the next 60 to 120 days and will be funded by the proceeds from our excess capital." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "Second quarter total revenues increased $177 million or 25%, while net income improved $5 million or 17%. Diluted EPS was $1.21 compared to $1.13." (CFO & Treasurer David Hisey)

Outlook

  • "At the onset of '26, we expect the existing home sales to improve around 6% to 8%. However... we now anticipate a much softer improvement with growth more likely topping around 2% when compared to last year, keeping us solidly in the low 4 million existing house sales range." (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)

  • "Even with those investments, I believe we can deliver earnings growth that will outpace revenue growth over the second half and for the full year for the overall company." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "If we stay flat, which I think we will, I don't think we'll see any growth in the RES market for the rest of the year. I believe that we'll grow earnings -- revenue probably 20% and earnings 30%." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • "The improvement in margin, I see is about 0.5 point for the company year-over-year, might be 0.4, might be 0.6." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • Change from the prior quarter: "All in, our view of the residential market growth will be closer probably to 3% to 5% for the year." (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger, Q1 2026) vs. "we now anticipate... growth more likely topping around 2%" (CEO & Director Eppinger, Q2 2026).

Financial Results

  • "Second quarter total revenues increased $177 million or 25%, while net income improved $5 million or 17%." (CFO & Treasurer David Hisey)

  • "On an adjusted basis, net income was $43 million or diluted earnings per share of $1.39 compared to $38 million and $1.34." (CFO & Treasurer Hisey)

  • "In our Title segment, operating revenues increased $91 million or 15%... Title operating expenses increased 17%... As a result, Title pretax income was comparable to last year." (CFO & Treasurer Hisey)

  • "On our agency operations, gross agency revenues increased 25% to $377 million from $301 million last year... net agency revenues increased $13 million or 26% compared to last year." (CFO & Treasurer Hisey)

  • "On our Real Estate Solutions segment, total revenues increased 75% to -- or $85 million... Real Estate Solutions adjusted pretax income more than doubled to $27 million from $12 million." (CFO & Treasurer Hisey)

  • "Total cash and investments were approximately $400 million in excess of statutory premium reserve requirements. Total Stewart stockholders' equity at June 30 was approximately $1.66 billion, representing a book value of approximately $55 per share." (CFO & Treasurer Hisey)

Q&A

  • Bose George, KBW: "can you just talk about the annualized margin outlook, especially if you remain in this higher for longer with mortgage rates at like 6.5%?" CEO Eppinger: "I believe that we'll grow earnings -- revenue probably 20% and earnings 30%... The improvement in margin, I see is about 0.5 point for the company year-over-year... those numbers do not include what I expect in the next 60 to 90 days" from acquisitions.

  • Bose George, KBW: "when we think about the scale, is it similar to MCS?" CEO Eppinger: "None of them are huge. So none of them are in the MCS size kind of category... I would guess that we'll be able to deploy the full amount of what we raised plus some in the next probably by the end of the year."

  • Bose George, KBW: "Was there any slippage of like large deals" in commercial fees? CEO Eppinger: "It is very lumpy... I don't see any momentum shift. The pipeline is good... we've gone from about 9% to we were about 13.5%, 14% share... I'd like to believe... we can get it to 20%."

  • Oscar Nieves Santana, Stephens: agency outgrowing direct—"competitive or mix pressure" on agent retention? CEO Eppinger: "we are shifting share nicely on the agency side. I don't see the dynamic within the agents changing anything" and added "inorganic opportunities... are emerging" including "one... in Texas" that "fills in Dallas."

  • Michael Rindos, StoneX: pricing competitiveness and timing in commercial? CEO Eppinger: "there really isn't a lot of price sensitivity" and on cycle times "you could have a 2-year deal" with costs incurred before closing, creating a lag during high growth.

  • Michael Rindos, StoneX: M&A pricing and competition? CEO Eppinger: "a title thing is somewhere between 4 to 6 EBITDA" and "If you have higher-margin service businesses that can get all the way to 8 EBITDA" and later added "The competitive nature of these transactions is very light."

  • Bose George, KBW: centralized title softness? CEO Eppinger: "That bulk business is very bouncy" and said the segment "probably saw a 20% reduction" with an earnings impact "in the $2 million or $3 million range," while noting open orders were "way up for the next quarter."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment slightly negative: questions centered on elevated expenses, sustainability of margins in a "higher for longer" rate backdrop, commercial fee volatility, and acquisition pricing/competition.

  • Management sentiment slightly positive: "I'm just thrilled about our momentum as a company" (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger), while framing reinvestment and M&A as deliberate and reiterating expectations for margin improvement and earnings leverage.

  • Versus last quarter, management language shifted from "closer probably to 3% to 5%" residential market growth (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger, Q1 2026) to "growth more likely topping around 2%" (CEO & Director Eppinger, Q2 2026), with Q&A more focused on expense levels and margin durability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Housing outlook softened: Q1 included "our view of the residential market growth will be closer probably to 3% to 5% for the year" (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger, Q1 2026) while Q2 moved to "growth more likely topping around 2%" (CEO & Director Eppinger, Q2 2026).

  • Investment posture intensified: Q2 introduced incremental talent spend detail—"around $8 million" in Q2 (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)—with expected payoff timing of "the next 2 to 4 quarters" (CEO & Director Eppinger).

  • M&A cadence became more time-bound: Q2 added "transactions that we anticipate will close in the next 60 to 120 days" (CEO & Director Eppinger), whereas Q1 described a broader pipeline and capital capacity without a near-term closing window.

  • Analyst focus shifted from segment growth/margins (commercial cadence and RES margin structure in Q1) to expense trajectory, margin durability under 6.5% rates, and the size/competition dynamics of acquisitions in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • "Interest rates remain a critical factor" and management cited a move "back up around 6.5%" in Q2 as a headwind for housing activity. (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)

  • Margin pressure from mix and scaling costs: "Our significant growth in real estate solutions and commercial activity... has resulted in an increase in our operating expense ratios." (CEO & Director Eppinger)

  • Commercial timing risk: "these deals are kind of going to be 3 quarters or so to a year" and can be "a little fickle about kind of timing of closing," creating cost timing mismatches. (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)

  • Centralized/bulk volatility: "That bulk business is very bouncy" and the quarter saw "a 20% reduction" with a "$2 million or $3 million" earnings impact. (CEO & Director Frederick Eppinger)

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized strong growth despite a housing market the CEO described as still in a "multiyear slump," while lowering its expectation for 2026 existing-home-sales growth to "around 2%." The CEO tied slower quarterly earnings growth to deliberate, incremental hiring investments and positioned further near-term M&A, saying deals are in process that should close in "60 to 120 days." The CFO highlighted higher revenues, improved net income, and strong Real Estate Solutions profitability, while management acknowledged operating expense ratios rising with business mix and the timing volatility inherent in commercial and bulk-centered operations.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gold plunges as spiking oil prices lift 10-year Treasurys to 18-month high
data.description:
Gold futures fell sharply on Thursday after reaching a two-week high in the previous session, as surging tensions in the Middle East drove crude oil and Treasury yields higher, adding to concerns over inflation and potential interest rate hikes.

Brent crude oil prices reached $100/bbl for the first time since May ‌after ⁠Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis said they attacked two Saudi oil tankers, raising fears that disruption to global oil supplies could spread beyond the Strait of Hormuz.

The oil spike helped lift the U.S. dollar, making greenback-priced bullion expensive for buyers overseas, while yields on the benchmark 10-year ​U.S. Treasury note rose to 4.703%, the highest since January 2025.

"Higher crude oil prices are ​pushing up bond yields on the notion that central banks will not be able to lower their interest rates because of problematic inflation," American Gold Exchange market analyst Jim Wyckoff said in a note. "Rising bond yields are the enemy of gold and silver ​market bulls because gold and silver carry no yield."

Investors ​are awaiting the ⁠Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and Chair Kevin Warsh's comments following the central bank's two-day policy meeting next week.

Traders are pricing in ​about an 83% chance of a Fed rate hike in September, up ​from 68% ⁠on Wednesday, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for July delivery plunged 2.4% to $4,046.60/oz, and front-month Comex July silver (XAGUSD:CUR) slumped 3.7% to $57.798/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SGDM), (SGDJ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Safety Insurance agrees to $1.54B Mapfre buyout deal; shares soar 35% in after-hours
data.description:
• Safety Insurance Group (SAFT) announced on Thursday that it has agreed to be acquired by an affiliate of Mapfre in an all-cash deal valued at ~$1.54B.
  • The shareholders will receive $105 per share in cash, representing a 44% premium to the stock's closing price near $72.94.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2027, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • After the deal closes, Safety Insurance will continue operating under its existing brand as a wholly owned subsidiary of Mapfre U.S.A.
  • CEO George Murphy said the deal delivers an exceptional outcome for shareholders and will provide greater financial strength, technology, and growth opportunities while maintaining Safety's commitment to customers and agents.
  • On the market side, the Safety Insurance stock surged over 35% to $99.01 in the after-hours trading session.

data.title: Stripe in talks to acquire AI startup OpenRouter for ~$10B - report
data.description:
Payments firm Stripe (STRIP) is in discussions to acquire the AI startup OpenRouter for about $10B, people familiar with the matter told The Wall Street Journal.

A transaction could be announced soon, according to the sources.

Other big tech companies had also been looking to acquire OpenRouter, the report published on Thursday noted.

OpenRouter sells software that allows customers to easily access AI models built by OpenAI (OPENAI) and Anthropic (ANTHRO) as well as free-to-download alternatives.

The firm's backers include CapitalG, the growth-stage venture capital fund of Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) (GOOG:CA).

The transaction is expected to help Stripe expand in the AI market, WSJ noted.

Recently, Stripe was also said to be planning to acquire ​PayPal (PYPL) (PYPL:CA) jointly with private equity firm Advent International in a $60.50 per share, or over $53B, deal.

However, PayPal's board reportedly sees the $53B takeover bid as undervaluing the company and facing regulatory and financing hurdles.

data.title: Ovintiv GAAP EPS of $1.62 misses by $0.49
data.description:
• Ovintiv press release (OVV): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.62 misses by $0.49.
  • Recognized a net gain on risk management in revenues of $122 million, before tax
  • Generated cash from operating activities of $1.6 billion and Non-GAAP Cash Flow of approximately $1.3 billion
  • Second quarter average total production volumes were approximately 615 MBOE/d, including 206 Mbbls/d of oil and condensate, 82 Mbbls/d of other NGLs (C2 to C4) and 1,959 MMcf/d of natural gas
  • Second quarter capital investment of $574 million was at the midpoint of the guidance range of $550 million to $600 million
  • Reported second quarter upstream operating expense of $3.25 per BOE, upstream transportation and processing costs of $9.47 per BOE, production, mineral and other taxes of $1.43 per BOE, or 3.5% of upstream product revenue
  • Excluding the impact of hedges, second quarter average realized price for oil and condensate was $97.50 per barrel (105% of WTI), $21.67 per barrel for other NGLs, and $1.71 per Mcf (59% of NYMEX) for natural gas, resulting in a total average realized price of $41.00 per BOE
  • Including the impact of hedges, second quarter average realized price for oil and condensate was $91.22 per barrel (98% of WTI), $21.67 per barrel for other NGLs, and $1.99 per Mcf (69% of NYMEX) for natural gas, resulting in a total average realized price of $39.79 per BOE

  • 2026 Guidance

    3Q 2026

    Full Year 2026

    Total Production (MBOE/d)

    615 – 640

    630 – 645

    Oil & Condensate (Mbbls/d) 

    205 – 210

    210 – 212

    NGLs (C2 to C4) (Mbbls/d)

    75 – 80

    83 – 85

    Natural Gas (MMcf/d)

    2,000 – 2,100

    2,025 – 2,075

    Capital Investment ($ Millions)

    $550 – $600

    $2,250 – $2,350
